findings.evidence: `rg -n "@\[expose\]"` hits `weightedDegreeZeroClassHom` at line 66, `weightedDegreeZeroQuotientEquivPicZero` at line 125, `degreeZeroClassHom` at line 172, `degreeZeroQuotientEquivWeightedDegreeZeroOne` at line 246, and `degreeZeroQuotientEquivUnweightedPicZero` at line 264; lines 81-93, 135-153, 180-192, 257-260, and 273-296 already provide the public apply/mk simp API.
findings.fix: Keep these bodies unexposed and export only the characteristic lemmas, or restructure the declarations so the apply/mk lemmas can be public without exposing the implementation bodies.
findings.issue: The public homs and equivalences are marked `@[expose]`, making their transport and first-isomorphism implementations part of the downstream API even though users are given normal-form lemmas.

summary: The quotient model has the expected characteristic lemmas, but it still exposes implementation bodies that consumers should not need to unfold.
